---
title: "elevation and z star"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(reactable)
library(patchwork)
library(tinytex)
 
# Load the dataset
#Make sure to use double \\ to avoid errors
rtk_data <- read_csv("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\All_Sites_Calculated (with z star).csv")

```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
# 1. Filter the master dataset to ONLY include sample 'a'
plot_data_all_a <- rtk_data %>%
  # grepl("a$", ...) ensures we only keep rows where Point_ID ends with 'a' or 'A'
  filter(grepl("a$", Point_ID, ignore.case = TRUE))

# 2. Create the Cross-Site Comparison Plot
cross_site_plot_z <- ggplot(plot_data_all_a, aes(x = Distance_AlongTransect_m, y = z_star, color = Transect_ID)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Location), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = Transect_ID), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.5) + 
  
  # Set up labels
  labs(
    title = "Cross-Site z* Value Comparison (Replicates 'a' Only)",
    subtitle = "Comparing z* value across San Pablo, Corte Madera, Bucks Landing, and Giant Marsh",
    x = "Distance Along Transect (m)",
    y = "z*",
    color = "Transect",
    shape = "Location"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  
  # Format the legend to make it easy to read
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  )

# 3. Display the plot
print(cross_site_plot_z)

# 4. Save the plot to your main Plots folder
overall_plots_folder <- "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images"

# Create the folder if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(overall_plots_folder)) dir.create(overall_plots_folder, recursive = TRUE)

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\All_Sites_Track_A_Comparison_z.png"),
  plot = cross_site_plot_z,
  width = 10,  # Keeping it wide to comfortably fit the legend
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

#cat("Successfully saved the overall comparison plot to:\n", overall_plots_folder, "\n")


#{r}
# 1. Filter the master dataset to ONLY include sample 'b'
plot_data_all_b <- rtk_data %>%
  # grepl("b$", ...) ensures we only keep rows where Point_ID ends with 'b' or 'B'
  filter(grepl("b$", Point_ID, ignore.case = TRUE))

# 2. Create the Cross-Site Comparison Plot for track 'b'
cross_site_plot_b_z <- ggplot(plot_data_all_b, aes(x = Distance_AlongTransect_m, y = z_star, color = Transect_ID)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Location), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = Transect_ID), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.5) + 
  
  # Set up labels
  labs(
    title = "Cross-Site Elevation Comparison (Samples 'b' Only)",
    subtitle = "Comparing z* Values across San Pablo, Corte Madera, Bucks Landing, and Giant Marsh",
    x = "Distance Along Transect (m)",
    y = "z*",
    color = "Transect",
    shape = "Location"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  
  # Keeping the same "Paired" palette so Transect colors remain consistent with the 'a' plot
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  
  # Format the legend to make it easy to read
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  )

# 3. Display the plot
print(cross_site_plot_b_z)

# 4. Save the plot to your main Plots folder
overall_plots_folder <- "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images"

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\All_Sites_Track_B_Comparison.png"),
  plot = cross_site_plot_b_z,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)



#{r}
# 1. Filter the master dataset to ONLY include sample 'c'
plot_data_all_c <- rtk_data %>%
  # grepl("c$", ...) ensures we only keep rows where Point_ID ends with 'c' or 'C'
  filter(grepl("c$", Point_ID, ignore.case = TRUE))

# 2. Create the Cross-Site Comparison Plot for track 'c'
cross_site_plot_c_z <- ggplot(plot_data_all_c, aes(x = Distance_AlongTransect_m, y = z_star, color = Transect_ID)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Location), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = Transect_ID), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.5) + 
  
  # Set up labels
  labs(
    title = "Cross-Site Elevation Comparison (Track 'c' Only)",
    subtitle = "Comparing z* values across San Pablo, Corte Madera, Bucks Landing, and Giant Marsh",
    x = "Distance Along Transect (m)",
    y = "z*",
    color = "Transect",
    shape = "Location"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  
  # Keeping the same "Paired" palette so Transect colors remain consistent
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  
  # Format the legend to make it easy to read
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  )

# 3. Display the plot in the notebook
print(cross_site_plot_c_z)

# 4. Save the plot to your main Plots folder
overall_plots_folder <- "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images"

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\imagesAll_Sites_Track_C_Comparison.png"),
  plot = cross_site_plot_c_z,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#{r}
# 1. Start with only track 'a' data
plot_data_a <- rtk_data %>%
  filter(grepl("a$", Rep, ignore.case = TRUE))

# 2. Split into Ramped and Scarped datasets
data_ramped <- plot_data_a %>%
  filter(Location %in% c("SanPablo", "BucksLanding"))

data_scarped <- plot_data_a %>%
  filter(Location %in% c("CorteMadera", "GiantMarsh"))

# 3. Create the Ramped Plot
plot_ramped_z <- ggplot(data_ramped, aes(x = Dist, y = z_star, color = Tran)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Site), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = Tran), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.5) + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "Ramped Sites (San Pablo & Bucks Landing)",
    x = "Distance Along Transect (m)",
    y = "z*",
    color = "Transect",
    shape = "Location"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  # --- NEW: Added guides to force Location on top (order = 1) ---
  guides(
    shape = guide_legend(order = 1),
    color = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",          
    legend.box = "vertical",             
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, face = "italic")
  )

# 4. Create the Scarped Plot
plot_scarped_z <- ggplot(data_scarped, aes(x = Dist, y = z_star, color = Tran)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Site), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = Tran), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.5) + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "Scarped Sites (Corte Madera & Giant Marsh)",
    x = "Distance Along Transect (m)",
    y = "z*",
    color = "Transect",
    shape = "Location"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  # --- NEW: Added guides to force Location on top (order = 1) ---
  guides(
    shape = guide_legend(order = 1),
    color = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",        
    legend.box = "vertical",
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, face = "italic")
  )

# 5. Combine the plots for side-by-side viewing
combined_morphology_plot_z <- (plot_ramped_z | plot_scarped_z) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Marsh Morphology Comparison: Ramped vs. Scarped Sites with z* Calculation",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
  ) &
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(5.0, 7.0)) # Ensures both graphs are on the exact same vertical scale

# 6. Display the combined plot
print(combined_morphology_plot_z)

# 7. Save the combined plot to your Overall Comparisons folder
overall_plots_folder <- "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images"
if (!dir.exists(overall_plots_folder)) dir.create(overall_plots_folder, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\Morphology_Comparison_Track_A_zstar.png"),
  plot = combined_morphology_plot_z,
  width = 12,  
  height = 7,  
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

```

```{r, separating the columns}

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

sed_thesis_keyed <- sed_thesis |>
  mutate(
    Site      = str_remove_all(as.character(Site), " "),
    Tran      = paste0("T", as.character(Tran)),
    Dist      = as.numeric(as.character(Dist)),
    Replicate = as.character(Replicate)
  )

rtk_detail <- rtk_data_detail |>
  mutate(
    Site      = as.character(Site),
    Tran      = as.character(Tran),
    Dist      = as.numeric(as.character(Dist)),
    Replicate = as.character(Replicate)
  ) |>
  select(Site, Tran, Dist, Replicate, Elevation_m, z_star)

sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Site));      sort(unique(rtk_detail$Site))
sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Tran));      sort(unique(rtk_detail$Tran))
sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Dist));      sort(unique(rtk_detail$Dist))
sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Replicate)); sort(unique(rtk_detail$Replicate))
```

```{r, combine the rtk database values with thesis_df}

library(dplyr)

elev_z_flux <- sed_thesis_keyed |>
  left_join(rtk_detail, by = c("Site", "Tran", "Dist", "Replicate"))

sum(!is.na(elev_z_flux$Elevation_m))
nrow(elev_z_flux)

# sediment rows with no RTK match
sed_thesis_keyed |>
  anti_join(rtk_detail, by = c("Site", "Tran", "Dist", "Replicate"))
```

```{r}

elev_z_flux <- sed_thesis_keyed |>
  left_join(rtk_detail, by = c("Site", "Tran", "Dist", "Replicate"))

sum(!is.na(elev_z_flux$Elevation_m))
nrow(elev_z_flux)

elev_z_flux |>
  filter(is.na(Elevation_m)) |>
  distinct(Site, Tran, Dist, Replicate)

rtk_detail |>
  filter(Site == "CorteMadera")   # swap in one of the unmatched Site values you saw above

sort(unique(sed_thesis$Site))
sort(unique(rtk_data_detail$Site))
sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Dist));      sort(unique(rtk_detail$Dist))
sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Tran));      sort(unique(rtk_detail$Tran))
sort(unique(sed_thesis_keyed$Replicate)); sort(unique(rtk_detail$Replicate))
```

```{r, basic play with z star and elevation}

flux_z_Site <-ggplot(elev_z_flux, aes(x=z_star, y=Flux, color=Site)) +
  geom_point() + geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.2, alpha = 0.6) + theme_minimal() + scale_y_log10() + facet_wrap(~ Study.Phase) + 
  labs(x = "z*", y = "Log Flux of Sediment Deposition (g/m^2)", 
       title = "Sediment Deposition Rates Across Distances Per Site Per Season")
flux_z_Site

ggsave("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\imagesflux_z_Site.png", 
       width = 60, 
       height = 40, 
       dpi = 300,
       limitsize = FALSE)

```

```{r}

flux_z_SP <-ggplot(elev_z_flux, aes(x=z_star, y=Flux, color=Study.Phase)) +
  geom_point() + geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.2, alpha = 0.6) + theme_minimal() + scale_y_log10() + facet_wrap(~ Site, scales = "free_y") + geom_smooth(aes(color = Study.Phase), method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) + 
  labs(x = "z*", y = "Log Flux of Sediment Deposition (g/m^2)", 
       title = "Sediment Deposition Rates Across Distances Per Site Per Season")
flux_z_SP

ggsave("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\imagesflux_z_SP.png", 
       width = 60, 
       height = 40, 
       dpi = 300,
       limitsize = FALSE)
```

```{r}
flux_z_detail = ggplot(elev_z_flux, aes(x = z_star, y = Flux)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Study.Phase), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, aes(color = Study.Phase), linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(Study.Phase ~ Site, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_bw()

flux_z_detail

ggsave("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_z_detail.png", 
       width = 8, 
       height = 5, 
       dpi = 300,
       limitsize = FALSE)


flux_z_smooth = ggplot(elev_z_flux, aes(x = z_star, y = Flux)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Study.Phase), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Site, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_bw()
flux_z_smooth

ggsave("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_z_smooth.png", 
       width = 8, 
       height = 5, 
       dpi = 300,
       limitsize = FALSE)
```

```{r, statistics}

elev_z_flux_clean <- elev_z_flux |> 
  filter(Flux > 0)  # removes negatives and zeros

# Double check it worked
sum(elev_z_flux_clean$Flux <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)  # should return 0
summary(elev_z_flux_clean$Flux)


# 1. Test if slopes are significantly different between morphology types
library(lme4)
model <- lmer(log(Flux) ~ z_star * marsh_edge_type + (1|Site) + (1|Study.Phase), 
              data = elev_z_flux_clean)
summary(model)


```

```{r, more statistics}

# 1. Get p-values formally
library(lmerTest)
model_full <- lmer(log(Flux) ~ z_star * marsh_edge_type + 
                   (1 | Site) + (1 | Study.Phase),  
                   data = elev_z_flux_clean,
                   REML = FALSE)
summary(model_full)  # lmerTest will now show p-values

# 2. Calculate R-squared
library(MuMIn)
r.squaredGLMM(model_full)
# R2m = variance explained by fixed effects only
# R2c = variance explained by entire model including random effects

# 3. Get predicted slopes per edge type for reporting
library(emmeans)
emtrends(model_full, ~ marsh_edge_type, var = "z_star")
```

```{r, more results again}

# 1. Get formal p-values for reporting
library(lmerTest)
summary(model_full)

# 2. Get R-squared values
library(MuMIn)
r.squaredGLMM(model_full)

# 3. Plot the predicted trends cleanly for a publication figure
library(ggeffects)
pred <- ggpredict(model_full, terms = c("z_star [all]", "marsh_edge_type"))

ggplot(pred, aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group, fill = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_point(data = elev_z_flux_clean, 
             aes(x = z_star, y = log(Flux), color = marsh_edge_type),
             alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#E69F00", "Scarped" = "#0072B2")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Ramped" = "#E69F00", "Scarped" = "#0072B2")) +
  labs(x = "z*", 
       y = "log(Sediment Flux g/m²)",
       color = "Edge Type", 
       fill  = "Edge Type",
       title = "Sediment Deposition by Marsh Edge Morphology") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

```{r}

library(ggdist)
library(ggplot2)

flux_sites_season = ggplot(elev_z_flux_clean, aes(x =z_star , y = log(Flux),
                               fill = marsh_edge_type,
                               color = marsh_edge_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, outlier.shape = NA,
               alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(0.5)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = marsh_edge_type),
             position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.15, dodge.width = 0.5),
             size = 1.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  ylim(-1, 6) +
  labs(y = "Log(Flux (g/m^3/day))", x = "Month of Study",
       fill = "Marsh Edge Type", color = "Marsh Edge Type",
       title = str_wrap("Sediment Deposition Rates Across Study Months Between Scarped and Ramped Marsh Edge Sites")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
flux_sites_season

ggsave("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_sites_season.png", 
       width = 8, 
       height = 5, 
       dpi = 300,
       limitsize = FALSE)

```

```{r, raincloud plots for season}

library(ggdist)
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(elev_z_flux_clean, aes(x = Study.Phase, y = log(Flux), 
                               fill = marsh_edge_type, 
                               color = marsh_edge_type)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5, width = 0.6, justification = -0.2, 
               .width = 0, point_colour = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_dots(side = "left", justification = 1.1, binwidth = 0.1, 
            dotsize = 0.5, alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Distance (m)", y = "log(Flux)", 
       fill = "Edge type", color = "Edge type",
       title = "Sediment flux distribution across distances by marsh edge type") +
  theme_bw() + facet_wrap(~ marsh_edge_type, ncol = 1)
```

```{r}
library(ggridges)

grouped_dist <- elev_z_flux_clean %>%
  group_by(Dist, marsh_edge_type) %>%
  summarise(Flux_dist = sum(Flux))

flux_raincloud = ggplot(grouped_dist, aes(x = Flux_dist, y = Dist, 
                               fill = marsh_edge_type,
                               color = marsh_edge_type)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.5, scale = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(x = "log(Flux (g/m^3/day))", y = "Distance from edge (m)",
       fill = "Edge type", color = "Edge type",
       title = str_wrap("Sediment Deposition Rates Across Distances from Scarped and Ramped Marsh Edges",width = 50)) +
  theme_bw()
flux_raincloud

ggsave("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_raincloud.png", 
    plot = flux_raincloud,    # always specify the plot explicitly
       width = 8, 
       height = 5, 
       units = "in",              # be explicit about units
       dpi = 300)



```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

summary_df <- elev_z_flux_clean |>
  group_by(Dist, marsh_edge_type) |>
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(log(Flux), na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(log(Flux), na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(Dist = as.numeric(as.character(Dist)))

flux_total_dist = ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = Dist, y = mean_flux, 
                        color = marsh_edge_type,
                        fill = marsh_edge_type)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_flux - se, ymax = mean_flux + se), 
              alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Ramped" = "#378ADD", "Scarped" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(x = "Distance from edge (m)", y = "Mean log(Flux)",
       fill = "Edge type", color = "Edge type",
       title = "Sediment deposition across marsh platform") +
  theme_bw()
flux_total_dist

ggsave("flux_total_dist.png", 
       plot = flux_total_dist,    # always specify the plot explicitly
       width = 8, 
       height = 5, 
       units = "in",              # be explicit about units
       dpi = 300)


#flux_total_dist <- flux_total_dist +
  #labs(
   # title = str_wrap("Sediment deposition across marsh platform by edge type and distance", width = 50),
  #  subtitle = str_wrap("Mean log flux with standard error shown as ribbon", width = 60)
  #) +
 # theme(
  #  plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 13),
  #  plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11, color = "gray40")
 # )
```


```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot flux (elevation/accretion surplus, mm/yr) vs z* for ONE site,
# with a linear trend line and R^2 annotation -- styled after Thorne et al.
#
# Expected input: a CSV with at least these columns:
#   site   : site/category name, e.g. "SFB Elevation", "SC Accretion"
#   z_star : position within tidal frame (x-axis)
#   flux   : elevation/accretion surplus rate, mm/yr (y-axis)
#
# Edit the CONFIG block below, then run the whole script (Ctrl+Shift+S
# or Cmd+Shift+S in RStudio, or step through with Ctrl+Enter).
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# install.packages(c("ggplot2", "dplyr"))  # uncomment to install if needed
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# ------------------------- CONFIG -------------------------
csv_path     <- "your_data.csv"      # path to your data file
"Site"  <- "site"               # column that identifies the site/group
site_to_plot <- "SFB Elevation"      # the single site you want to isolate
x_column     <- "z_star"             # z* column name
y_column     <- "flux"               # flux column name
# ------------------------------------------------------------


if (!"CorteMadera" %in% elev_z_flux[["Site"]]) {
  stop(paste0(
    "No rows found for site '", "CorteMadera", "'. ",
    "Available sites: ", paste(unique(elev_z_flux[["Site"]]), collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

c_df <- elev_z_flux %>%
  filter(.data[["Site"]] == "CorteMadera") %>%
  select(x = all_of("z_star"), y = all_of("Flux")) %>%
  na.omit()

# --- Fit linear model and get R^2 ---
fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = c_df)
r_squared <- summary(fit)$r.squared
slope     <- coef(fit)[2]
intercept <- coef(fit)[1]
p_value   <- summary(fit)$coefficients[2, 4]

cat("Site:", "CorteMadera", "\n")
cat("n =", nrow(c_df), "\n")
cat(sprintf("slope = %.4f, intercept = %.4f\n", slope, intercept))
cat(sprintf("R^2 = %.4f, p = %.4g\n", r_squared, p_value))

# --- Build plot ---
r2_label <- paste0("R^2 == ", round(r_squared, 2))

p <- ggplot(c_df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#3b6fa0", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = min(c_df$x) + 0.05 * diff(range(c_df$x)),
    y = max(c_df$y) * 0.9,
    label = r2_label,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = 0,
    size = 5,
    color = "dimgray"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "CorteMadera",
    x = "z*",
    y = "Flux"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#e9e9e9", color = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "white"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p)

# Save to file
ggsave("flux_vs_zstar.png", plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 200)
```


```{r, for san pablo }

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot flux (elevation/accretion surplus, mm/yr) vs z* for ONE site,
# with a linear trend line and R^2 annotation -- styled after Thorne et al.
#
# Expected input: a CSV with at least these columns:
#   site   : site/category name, e.g. "SFB Elevation", "SC Accretion"
#   z_star : position within tidal frame (x-axis)
#   flux   : elevation/accretion surplus rate, mm/yr (y-axis)
#
# Edit the CONFIG block below, then run the whole script (Ctrl+Shift+S
# or Cmd+Shift+S in RStudio, or step through with Ctrl+Enter).
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# install.packages(c("ggplot2", "dplyr"))  # uncomment to install if needed
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# ------------------------- CONFIG -------------------------
csv_path     <- "your_data.csv"      # path to your data file
"Site"  <- "site"               # column that identifies the site/group
site_to_plot <- "SFB Elevation"      # the single site you want to isolate
x_column     <- "z_star"             # z* column name
y_column     <- "flux"               # flux column name
# ------------------------------------------------------------


if (!"SanPablo" %in% elev_z_flux[["Site"]]) {
  stop(paste0(
    "No rows found for site '", "SanPablo", "'. ",
    "Available sites: ", paste(unique(elev_z_flux[["Site"]]), collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

s_df <- elev_z_flux %>%
  filter(.data[["Site"]] == "SanPablo") %>%
  select(x = all_of("z_star"), y = all_of("Flux")) %>%
  na.omit()

# --- Fit linear model and get R^2 ---
fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = s_df)
r_squared <- summary(fit)$r.squared
slope     <- coef(fit)[2]
intercept <- coef(fit)[1]
p_value   <- summary(fit)$coefficients[2, 4]

cat("Site:", "CorteMadera", "\n")
cat("n =", nrow(s_df), "\n")
cat(sprintf("slope = %.4f, intercept = %.4f\n", slope, intercept))
cat(sprintf("R^2 = %.4f, p = %.4g\n", r_squared, p_value))

# --- Build plot ---
r2_label <- paste0("R^2 == ", round(r_squared, 2))

p <- ggplot(s_df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#3b6fa0", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = min(s_df$x) + 0.05 * diff(range(s_df$x)),
    y = max(s_df$y) * 0.9,
    label = r2_label,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = 0,
    size = 5,
    color = "dimgray"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "San Pablo",
    x = "z*",
    y = "Flux"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#e9e9e9", color = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "white"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p)

# Save to file
ggsave("flux_vs_zstar_sites.png", plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 200)
```



```{r, for giant marsh }


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot flux (elevation/accretion surplus, mm/yr) vs z* for ONE site,
# with a linear trend line and R^2 annotation -- styled after Thorne et al.
#
# Expected input: a CSV with at least these columns:
#   site   : site/category name, e.g. "SFB Elevation", "SC Accretion"
#   z_star : position within tidal frame (x-axis)
#   flux   : elevation/accretion surplus rate, mm/yr (y-axis)
#
# Edit the CONFIG block below, then run the whole script (Ctrl+Shift+S
# or Cmd+Shift+S in RStudio, or step through with Ctrl+Enter).
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# install.packages(c("ggplot2", "dplyr"))  # uncomment to install if needed
#library(ggplot2)
#library(dplyr)

# ------------------------- CONFIG -------------------------
csv_path     <- "your_data.csv"      # path to your data file
"Site"  <- "site"               # column that identifies the site/group
site_to_plot <- "SFB Elevation"      # the single site you want to isolate
x_column     <- "z_star"             # z* column name
y_column     <- "flux"               # flux column name
# ------------------------------------------------------------


if (!"GiantMarsh" %in% elev_z_flux[["Site"]]) {
  stop(paste0(
    "No rows found for site '", "GiantMarsh", "'. ",
    "Available sites: ", paste(unique(elev_z_flux[["Site"]]), collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

g_df <- elev_z_flux %>%
  filter(.data[["Site"]] == "GiantMarsh") %>%
  select(x = all_of("z_star"), y = all_of("Flux")) %>%
  na.omit()

# --- Fit linear model and get R^2 ---
fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = g_df)
r_squared <- summary(fit)$r.squared
slope     <- coef(fit)[2]
intercept <- coef(fit)[1]
p_value   <- summary(fit)$coefficients[2, 4]

cat("Site:", "GiantMarsh", "\n")
cat("n =", nrow(s_df), "\n")
cat(sprintf("slope = %.4f, intercept = %.4f\n", slope, intercept))
cat(sprintf("R^2 = %.4f, p = %.4g\n", r_squared, p_value))

# --- Build plot ---
r2_label <- paste0("R^2 == ", round(r_squared, 2))

p <- ggplot(g_df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#3b6fa0", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = min(g_df$x) + 0.05 * diff(range(s_df$x)),
    y = max(g_df$y) * 0.9,
    label = r2_label,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = 0,
    size = 5,
    color = "dimgray"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Giant Marsh",
    x = "z*",
    y = "Flux"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#e9e9e9", color = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "white"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p)

# Save to file
ggsave("flux_vs_zstar_g_sites.png", plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 200)
```


```{r, for buck's landing}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot flux (elevation/accretion surplus, mm/yr) vs z* for ONE site,
# with a linear trend line and R^2 annotation -- styled after Thorne et al.
#
# Expected input: a CSV with at least these columns:
#   site   : site/category name, e.g. "SFB Elevation", "SC Accretion"
#   z_star : position within tidal frame (x-axis)
#   flux   : elevation/accretion surplus rate, mm/yr (y-axis)
#
# Edit the CONFIG block below, then run the whole script (Ctrl+Shift+S
# or Cmd+Shift+S in RStudio, or step through with Ctrl+Enter).
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# install.packages(c("ggplot2", "dplyr"))  # uncomment to install if needed
#library(ggplot2)
#library(dplyr)


if (!"Buck'sLanding" %in% elev_z_flux[["Site"]]) {
  stop(paste0(
    "No rows found for site '", "Buck'sLanding", "'. ",
    "Available sites: ", paste(unique(elev_z_flux[["Site"]]), collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

b_df <- elev_z_flux %>%
  filter(.data[["Site"]] == "Buck'sLanding") %>%
  select(x = all_of("z_star"), y = all_of("Flux")) %>%
  na.omit()

# --- Fit linear model and get R^2 ---
fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = b_df)
r_squared <- summary(fit)$r.squared
slope     <- coef(fit)[2]
intercept <- coef(fit)[1]
p_value   <- summary(fit)$coefficients[2, 4]

cat("Site:", "CorteMadera", "\n")
cat("n =", nrow(s_df), "\n")
cat(sprintf("slope = %.4f, intercept = %.4f\n", slope, intercept))
cat(sprintf("R^2 = %.4f, p = %.4g\n", r_squared, p_value))

# --- Build plot ---
r2_label <- paste0("R^2 == ", round(r_squared, 2))

p <- ggplot(b_df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#3b6fa0", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = min(b_df$x) + 0.05 * diff(range(s_df$x)),
    y = max(b_df$y) * 0.9,
    label = r2_label,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = 0,
    size = 5,
    color = "dimgray"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Buck's Landing",
    x = "z*",
    y = "Flux"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#e9e9e9", color = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "white"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p)

# Save to file
ggsave("flux_vs_zstar_b_sites.png", plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 200)
```


```
